mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as investors price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines month-over-month but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of such observations over a 3-year rolling window, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5%, it signals aggressive tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5% over a 5-year period, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable or declining interest rates signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts construction and consumer spending, but if rates are also rising the signal becomes negative due to affordability erosion; the joint interaction predicts negative returns late-cycle.
failure_condition: If the quartile of observations with highest HOUST growth and highest FEDFUNDS levels does not produce average negative 20-day returns significantly different from zero (t-test p<0.05) over a 10-year sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the early post-recession recovery phase, industrial production gains reflect pent-up demand and supply chain normalization, directly boosting cyclical sector earnings and producing short-term positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances during NBER-defined recovery months over two cycles, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues and earnings, thus supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the consumption channel materializes.
failure_condition: If RSAFS grows at an annualized rate above 3% but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such episodes over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A climb in the unemployment rate above 5% signals economic slack and rising recession risk, which depresses earnings expectations and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 5% but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate rise concurrently, it indicates a stagflationary mix of cost-push pressure and monetary tightening that severely hurts equity valuations through margin compression and higher discount rates, producing negative returns.
failure_condition: If the coincident rise of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS (both in top quartile of their 2-year history) does not result in negative average 20-day SPX returns below -1% over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In early expansion regimes, accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strong aggregate demand and rising corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as markets price in sustained expansion.
failure_condition: If quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annualized but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of quarters over a 20-year sample, hypothesis fails.
